name: eng-org
description: 5-role multi-agent engineering org for any project — EM → Tech Leads → Domain Devs → independent Test agents → specialist Reviewer agents, plus a system Architect that produces versioned ADRs before decomposition. /eng-org:init reads your project, detects stack and domains, then sets up tailored agents, slash commands, governance files with 9 binding guardrails, and a portable context layer (AGENTS.md universal entry point, machine-readable eng-org.json manifest, dated DECISIONS.md logs, a multi-project scaffolder, and the /eng-org:graphyfy skill that draws Mermaid linking-graphs of the whole project) so any AI or engineer can pick the project up cold. 0.12.0: Mode L autopilot — an autonomous build-until-done loop with G-9 clarity gate, crash-safe external driver, fingerprint circuit breaker, and an immutable SPEC/acceptance-criteria zone against reward hacking. New in 0.13.0: GR deep-review inside /eng-org:run-reviews — the gr multi-specialist engine reviews the REQ's diff in local-diff mode (gr review --range base..HEAD; any base branch, no GitHub PR, no token, nothing posted), with zero-install auto-resolution of the gr binary (scripts/gr-ensure.sh; no gr-reviewer plugin required, graceful skip when offline), a mandatory TL evidence-verification gate over every GR finding (CONFIRMED/FALSE-POSITIVE/OUT-OF-SCOPE dispositions in gr-review.md, enforced by merge-readiness), and a learning loop that appends every confirmed finding to governance/MISTAKES.md. New in 0.14.0: Tier 1 token-optimization v2 — report diet v2 (verdict-carrying reports capped at ~40-line prose on GREEN/APPROVE, unbounded on RED/BLOCK; evidence paths mandatory in frontmatter; TL forced-sampling audit every REQ; dev-reports/gr-review/em-summary/retro/merge-readiness exempt), incremental fix-iterations v2 (scripts/invalidation.mjs computes transitive-import-closure BFS to pin unaffected tiers as GREEN@<sha> (pinned) with audit trail; reviewer-security/test-regression/G-7/GR always re-run; mistakes_sha256 hash-guards MISTAKES.md staleness), context pack v2 (dedicated context-packer agent authors verbatim tag-sliced extracts per REQ; GUARDRAILS.md always included whole; exclusion manifest mandatory; raw-doc escape hatch logged; rotating canary reviewer reads raw and files pack_audit verdict; test-regression and GR always read raw). New in 0.15.0: review-pipeline hardening + token diet round-2 — mechanical verdict-lint (scripts/verdict-lint.mjs parses every TASK-*-review-*.md, derives the verdict from the max confirmed finding severity, and hard-errors on mismatch in run-reviews + merge-readiness; SKIP-with-note stubs supported), MISTAKES.md learning-loop automation (scripts/mistakes-gate.mjs enforces a dated-heading entry grammar and blocks merge-readiness when a REQ's confirmed findings produced no MISTAKES entry), reviewer fan-out consolidation driven by an empirical overlap audit (new reviewer-governance + reviewer-domain-validator roles; security checks folded into the surviving roster; reviewer-indexes demoted to escalation-only Step 2b), fix-iteration focused re-review (Step 4b re-runs only the reviewers whose findings the fix touched, with explicit RESOLVED/UNRESOLVED re-verdicts per finding), and a quality-neutral token-reduction bundle (agent-file context diet round-2 with byte census, model pins — haiku on context-packer/test-regression with an explicit never-downgrade list — and report-template slimming). New in 0.16.0: measured intelligence jump (bench Intelligence Index 79.71 → 91.31, first release to clear the ≥89 target) from a multi-candidate calibration program — extraction-layer verdict enforcement (scripts/verdict-lint.mjs derives category + WARN ceilings from finding text so over-blocking is fixed where the input is mis-classified, not just in reviewer prose), a mechanical MISTAKES.md learning gate (scripts/mistakes-gate.mjs), two new reviewer roles (reviewer-governance + reviewer-domain-validator) with severity-tier calibration examples, an emit-time output-cap module (scripts/output-cap.mjs) with a frozen tier parameter (config/output-cap-parameter.json), and reviewer-depth increases across the roster. verdict_accuracy .636 → .939, precision .528 → .864, recall 1.000 held, guardrail 100% held. New in 0.17.0: the "best-plugin" Phase-1 process layer (Tracks A+B+C+G) — a mandatory TRD design stage before code (templates/trd.template.md freezes the 5-section Ratio TRD verbatim + eng-org extensions E1 design-principles-applied / E2 blast-radius change-budget / E3 file-by-file change map / E4 test-tier strategy, a mandatory Mermaid diagram, scripts/trd-lint.mjs, and a new /eng-org:trd command sequenced between tl-analyze and tl-assign with an EM approval gate); a design-principles curriculum (governance/DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md teaches 15 principles as def→why→smell→fix→when-NOT trade-offs, scripts/design-lint.mjs for objective mechanical smells, a Dev self-checklist + reviewer design-axis); an anti-rewrite / scope-discipline guard (scripts/scope-explosion-guard.mjs blocks a diff that exceeds the TRD change-budget by >1.5×, GUARDRAILS G-10 "Boy Scout not Demolition" applies design rules only to new/changed code with pre-existing issues logged to TECH_DEBT, full rewrites human-gated); and four DX commands (/eng-org:status table board, /eng-org:why prints the blocker + exact fix command, /eng-org:ship one-shot pipeline, /eng-org:blockers human inbox). Bench: no quality regression vs 0.16.0 — Intelligence Index 91.31 → 94.63, every sub-metric equal-or-better, output tokens −6.1%. Stops at production-ready PR approval — merges and deployment stay human-controlled.
